rationale.edition: ISSUE-MODE — no PR diff to classify. Issue-mode targets are always OSS by design; the batch caller filters by the OSS repo's Needs-Automation label. The bug reporter notes 'Edition: Enterprise (UI shared with OSS)', confirming the UI is shared and the fix lands in the OSS codebase.
rationale.skip: Not skipping. This is issue-mode (always needs_e2e=true) and the bug — a preview popup overflowing off the right edge of the viewport — is a user-facing UI defect fully testable via Playwright (hover + viewport boundary assertions). No skip marker, label, or comment present.
rationale.e2e: E2E is warranted. The bug describes a visual regression on the Pipelines list page: hovering over the View (eye) action shows a pipeline graph preview that overflows past the right viewport edge. This is a concrete, reproducible UI behavior that Playwright can verify by hovering over a pipeline row's View action and asserting the resulting popup is fully within the viewport bounds. Regression coverage will protect this fix going forward.
rationale.area: Pipelines — the bug title and body explicitly refer to the 'Pipelines page' and the 'View' (eye icon) action on pipeline rows. The Pipelines testfolder in playwright.yml already contains 12 sibling specs covering pipeline list, pipeline core, conditions, import, history, etc., making this the natural area for the new regression spec.
rationale.group: Pipelines — this is the existing playwright.yml matrix shard (testfolder) that runs all Pipeline-related specs (pipelines.spec.js, pipeline-core.spec.js, pipeline-dynamic.spec.js, etc.). The new spec tests the pipeline list page's preview popup, which belongs in the same shard.
rationale.existing_tests: No E2E specs in the diff — this is issue-mode (no code change, no PR diff). No existing Playwright test covers this specific overflow regression.
